held.basis: 'Im(omega_2) = -2^(3/4)*K(k_dual^2)' is a period-normalization control pinning 'the Legendre lattice normalization' of E_lambda.
the framework diagnoses 'constraint-surface geometry ... of a defining surface F=0', instantiated concretely as F=D²+DS+P²−3 in CALC-02/CALC-23.
K(sqrt u,sqrt gamma) is built as a degree-4 extension of K=F(u), the stem field of the S_5 quintic (four-charge Galois core): [K(sqrt u,sqrt gamma):K]=4.
Def 1.3 states the general parity-fixed convention (invariance under x->-x), then gives the specific inverse-channel pattern g_x=Bx^2+O(x^4), g_y=Ax^{-2}+O(1) satisfying it.

Def 7.1 builds S_R and N_R by 'Expand the smooth coefficients in (3.2)' — the Newton polyhedron is a combinatorial support/convex-hull reduction of the divisor-channel decomposition's coefficient data
GATE-A shows the fixed keystone kappa_c/kappa_int/kappa_s labels arise as the r=2 evaluation Ĉ_2 of the general bordered-minor channel-density polynomial Ĉ_r(t,u).
'This curvature reduction is the order-2 projection of that theorem [8.1′]' — the six ordered role charts are the order-2 instance of the general S₃ role-jet orbit carrier.
Under Ftilde=mu F, on F=0: Htilde = H+G, G=ga^T+ag^T, a=grad log mu -- the gauge transport law transforming the Hessian split.
H=(C_2)^k is the Galois/deck group of E/F_0(u); X is the smooth projective model of E, so H acts on X as deck transformations.
"k=4, degree 5: banked S_5 certificate" is the k=4 instance of the general N_k(u;M) norm-form family.
K is T-invariant (Thm part i) and for kappa>0 is a complete invariant separating S_3-orbits of triples (Cor. Nonlinear domains), i.e. K factors through the orbit space of triples.
Def. Coupling kernel: K=(K_spec, K_hol, kappa) is a tuple; K_spec is the projection/image of K onto its spectral-data (Jacobian) component.
Eq (3.2) gives exact sequence 1->W^vee->Gal(L/F)->G->1; section 9 sets G=S_5 (the four-charge Galois core) as the base of the resulting C_2^2 wreath S_5 group.
'Label the five embeddings of K into E by i=1,...,5' where E is the S_5-Galois splitting field of the four-charge core (2.5), giving u_i,gamma_i,beta_i,delta_i.
R_c=kappa_c/K_G etc: the barycentric coordinates are ratios of the keystone channel labels kappa_c,kappa_s,kappa_int by the total curvature K_G, per section 14.

I_epsilon is epsilon-parametrized (1.6); the primary channel epsilon=+1 gives I_primary, e.g. 'I_{\rm primary}=\frac12\int_{\Gamma_+}\Theta-2\pi' (7.4).  [harvest+verified: Source (U-1822, DBP_LANDEN_
